description: Evaluation set for the frontend skill integration (approach A: shared layer + sibling mode skills vs approach B: single router skill). Three eval families: (1) trigger_evals — does the right skill/mode activate? (2) cost_evals — token load per scenario, declarative file sets the script counts; (3) quality_evals — rubric checks that the shared content covers the key concepts (lightweight; real LLM quality needs an API). Token counting methodology in eval-methodology.md.

tokenizer.approximation: Claude uses a different BPE; cl100k_base is an approximation. It is accurate enough for the relative A-vs-B-vs-baseline comparison this eval exists to make. Absolute numbers are indicative, not exact.
tokenizer.fallback: If gpt-tokenizer is unavailable, the script falls back to chars/4 and tags the result as estimated.

trigger_evals.query: Build me a landing page for a SaaS analytics tool
This dashboard looks AI-generated, clean it up
Audit App.tsx for AI design tells, don't change anything
Make a hero section with shimmer buttons and aurora background
De-slop this pricing section, it has gradient text and glassmorphism
Convert this PPTX to a web presentation
trigger_evals.notes: Presentation product → slides skill, not the design cluster

trigger_evals.query: This sections/workspace directory is a mess, reorganize it
trigger_evals.notes: Structure reorg → code-organization skill, not design

trigger_evals.query: I have a URL, rebuild that site from scratch

shared_on_demand.description: The 7 files both approaches load on demand for a Next.js design task (1 stack adapter + 6 references). Counted once even when both modes run in one session.

cost_evals.description: Single build task: create a landing page on a Next.js project. Measures the dispatch cost + on-demand shared refs.
Single redesign task: de-slop an existing component. Measures dispatch cost + on-demand shared refs.
Both modes in one session: build a surface then audit/rewrite it. Shared refs counted once. This is where B's shared router may pay off (one router vs two mode SKILLs).
Trigger overhead only — what the agent pays just by activating the skill, before loading any reference. Isolates the A-vs-B dispatch delta.

baseline.description: Pre-integration state: the 6 source design skills fully loaded (SKILL.md + all their reference files) for one design task. Represents the 'before' the integration replaces. taste-skill (a 13-skill collection) is measured separately because it is not 1:1 comparable.

quality_evals.name: The anti-slop catalog exists in exactly one place
quality_evals.prompt: Verify the AI-tell catalog (purple-blue gradient, Inter, rounded-2xl+shadow-lg, glassmorphism, card sickness) is defined once in frontend-shared/references/design-language.md and not duplicated across frontend-build, frontend-redesign, frontend-studio.

verify.check: frontend-build/SKILL.md references it by path, does not re-list tells
frontend-redesign/SKILL.md references it by path, does not re-list tells

quality_evals.name: One canonical delivery checklist
quality_evals.prompt: Verify quality-checklist.md is the single checklist and the mode skills point to it.

verify.check: no mode SKILL.md inlines its own full checklist

quality_evals.name: Stack assumptions are adapters, not hardcoded
quality_evals.prompt: Verify no mode skill hardcodes a single framework; each points to stacks/*.md and fe-foundation outranks on conflict.

verify.check: frontend-build & frontend-redesign & frontend-studio all reference stacks/ via detection table
no skill body assumes SvelteKit-only or Next-only as the sole truth

quality_evals.name: A and B load the same reference content
quality_evals.prompt: Verify A (mode skills) and B (router+modes) point to the same frontend-shared/references files, so the token comparison is a fair test of dispatch structure, not content volume.

verify.check: frontend-build/SKILL.md and frontend-studio/modes/build.md reference the same 6 references
frontend-redesign/SKILL.md and frontend-studio/modes/redesign.md reference the same 6 references
